panels.title: RPC p95 Duration
panels.description: Shows the 95th-percentile RPC call duration in milliseconds. An upward trend means most users are starting to feel latency, not just outlier requests.

panels.title: RPC p99 Duration
panels.description: Shows the 99th-percentile RPC call duration in milliseconds. This captures worst-case latency; sustained increases may indicate resource contention or GC pauses.

panels.title: RSS Memory
panels.description: Shows the resident set size, representing physical memory actively used by the process. Steady growth without plateau may indicate a memory leak.

panels.title: Virtual Memory
panels.description: Shows total virtual address space reserved by the process. A large gap between virtual and RSS memory is normal, but runaway growth can exhaust address space limits.

panels.title: Messaging Publish Duration p50/p95/p99
panels.description: Shows median, 95th, and 99th percentile durations for publishing messages to queues. Rising values suggest broker back-pressure or network congestion.

panels.title: RPC Request Rate
panels.description: Tracks RPC calls per second over time. Correlate traffic dips or spikes with deployment events or upstream changes to identify causation.

panels.title: Process CPU Time by State
panels.description: Plots CPU time split by state (user, system) over time. A rising system-time share relative to user time may indicate excessive syscalls or context switching.

panels.title: Open File Descriptors
panels.description: Tracks the number of open file descriptors over time. A steady upward trend without leveling off suggests descriptor leaks that may eventually hit OS limits.

panels.title: Process Uptime
panels.description: Shows how long the process has been running since its last restart. Frequent resets indicate crash loops or aggressive restart policies.
